mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 65 percent of instances where unemployment exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 over 5-year out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects capacity utilization peaks that precede earnings acceleration, generating positive monthly S&P 500 returns before inflation forces policy tightening.
failure_condition: Signal reverses to negative returns during late-cycle phases or fails to beat baseline in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 3.0 percent increase discount rates and constrain credit availability, causing immediate negative repricing in equity valuations over the following five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of high-rate observations, invalidating the discount rate transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling total nonfarm payrolls signals structural labor market deterioration, which suppresses corporate revenue growth and yields negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint labor signal shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales volumes directly translate into increased corporate cash flows and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth fails to correlate with 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 110 during post-shock recovery phases indicates persistent supply-side bottlenecks and weak manufacturing demand, triggering negative short-term equity repricing.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in over 65 percent of low-production observations within the specified regime.
